description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly gray coloration interspersed with lighter, almost white, circular and streaked mineral inclusions. The surface is uneven, featuring both flat and jagged areas, and displays a natural, weathered texture typical of stones found in outdoor urban environments. Subtle layers and banding are visible, suggesting sedimentary origins, while the stone's edges are mostly blunt but not sharply defined. The coloration varies from dark gray to pale beige, with some areas showing a mottled or marbled effect. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in parks, sidewalks, or landscaped urban spaces.
description: A rugged, gray stone with lighter mineral streaks and a weathered, uneven surface, featuring natural banding and mottling.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the stone, highlighting its layered texture and mottled gray-white coloration.
description_view_1: This side reveals a more angular, upright profile with pronounced banding and a steeper edge, emphasizing the stone's height.
description_view_2: Here, the stone appears elongated and tapers towards one end, with visible mineral inclusions and a rough, jagged edge.
description_view_3: This angle displays a slanted, blocky face with subtle layering and a mix of dark and light patches, giving a sense of mass and solidity.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular banding and mottling with circular mineral inclusions, creating a natural, layered appearance.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with minor surface smoothing, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, often partially embedded or surrounded by low vegetation.
